subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral + independent (IMF, World Bank, IRENA, Freedom House, RSF, ISS) + international press.
One of Africa's freest small democracies (consistently 'Free', peaceful turnovers), shaken by a November 2022 attempted coup and credible reports of custody abuse of suspects.

Standout features: strong governance-for-income and a free press, against deep structural poverty, a chronic electricity crisis (EMAE diesel blackouts), import/aid dependence, and unmaterialized offshore oil. Net positive on institutional strength.

economic.notes: A reform-anchored but aid-dependent, tiny, import-reliant economy under a euro peg.

fiscal_state.notes: Fragile but reform-anchored under the IMF ECF, reaching a zero domestic primary balance in 2024 but heavily aid-financed with declining ODA. (IMF ECF 2nd review Dec 2025)

monetary_independence.notes: Essentially none - the dobra is pegged to the euro under the 2010 Portugal agreement, importing ECB policy. (IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The dobra is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Eased to single digits (core ~4.7% y/y May 2025) under a tight stance and the peg. (IMF 2025 Art IV)

banking_stability.notes: A small, thin banking sector - stable but shallow with limited supervision capacity. (IMF; inference)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Tiny and undiversified (cocoa, modest tourism), structurally import-dependent with a chronic deficit and no oil revenue. (World Bank; IMF)

institutional.notes: Functioning courts and a free press, with shallow institutions and a post-coup abuse blemish.

rule_of_law.notes: Functioning courts and broadly respected rights, but shallow institutions and post-coup custody-abuse allegations are a real blemish. (Freedom House 2024; ISS)

statistical_integrity.notes: Fully IMF-engaged with regular reviews, but very limited microstate capacity. (IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Weak and thin, with SOE inefficiency (notably EMAE) a flagged reform priority. (World Bank Aug 2025)

press_freedom.notes: Constitutionally guaranteed and largely respected, among Africa's more open environments. (Freedom House; RSF)

political_social.notes: Repeated peaceful alternations and protected liberties, with latent coup-attempt fragility.

political_stability.notes: Repeated peaceful alternations and a presidential election due by July 2026, but the 2022 coup attempt shows latent fragility. (Africa Center; IPU)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: No notable systemic abuse, but very limited data for a tiny, homogeneous state. (inference)

social_cohesion.notes: Generally cohesive, strained by ~13% below the international poverty line and emigration pressure. (World Bank 2024)

demographics.notes: Tiny (~230k) with high emigration of skilled workers and limited domestic opportunity. (World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: A stable, non-aligned, Lusophone-linked, conflict-free state.

alliance_reliability.notes: Stable, non-aligned, close to Portugal/the Lusophone bloc - predictable but inconsequential. (observed)

conflict_involvement.notes: No armed conflict, aside from the isolated 2022 coup attempt. (observed)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: No sanctions exposure, broadly open under the euro peg. (IMF)

physical_practical.notes: A chronic electricity crisis as the binding constraint, with weak healthcare and SIDS climate exposure.

climate.notes: An equatorial-Atlantic SIDS - coastal/sea-level erosion plus freshwater and rainfall shifts, tiny adaptive capacity. (ND-GAIN; IPCC SIDS)

healthcare.notes: Weak and donor-dependent, materially constrained by unreliable electricity. (IRENA 2024; WHO)

infrastructure.notes: The defining weakness - chronic blackouts from ~30 MW of inefficient diesel/hydro capacity (EMAE). (IEA; World Bank)

crime_safety.notes: Comparatively safe with low violent crime. (observed)

disaster_insurance.notes: Negligible penetration and minimal fiscal headroom for shocks. (inference)

summary: A rare, durable small-island democracy - consistently 'Free', with a relatively open press and repeated peaceful turnovers - but its institutions are shallow and were tested by a November 2022 coup attempt and subsequent custody-abuse allegations. Economically it is tiny, poor, and structurally import- and aid-dependent, with the chronic electricity crisis (inefficient EMAE diesel plants) the single biggest brake on growth; long-promised offshore oil has not materialized. The euro-pegged dobra delivers an inflation anchor at the cost of monetary autonomy, while an on-track IMF ECF program is stabilizing the fiscal/external position. Net skew is positive on the strength of governance, with material physical/economic fragility.
